original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.version: NICE clinical guideline CG126, last updated 25 August 2016
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with a clinician-established diagnosis of stable angina.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Outpatient stable-angina information, treatment-response review, and clinician-led investigation or revascularisation discussion.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for documenting established stable-angina symptom course, daily impact, concerns, treatment response and adverse effects, and clinician-entered follow-up. It does not diagnose angina, infer stability, recommend a medicine or dose, or determine revascularisation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — information, symptom course, daily impact, concerns, and support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting established stable-angina symptom context, provoking factors, daily impact, expectations, sudden worsening already reported, and clinician discussion.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.3–1.4.5 — medicine information, adverse effects, and response review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting an already prescribed treatment, adherence discussion, adverse effects, and clinician review of response; no medicine or dose is generated.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.6–1.5.12 — clinician-led revascularisation discussion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording a completed clinician discussion of relative risks, benefits, coronary anatomy, comorbidities, and preferences without recommending CABG or PCI.

title: Stable angina: management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
population: Adults with a clinician-established diagnosis of stable angina.
intended_setting: Outpatient stable-angina information, treatment-response review, and clinician-led investigation or revascularisation discussion.
